High inflation has led to a sharp increase in collective labor agreements and higher fuel and financing costs since 2021. And that is also the picture for 2026. The Dutch road transport sector has seen an unprecedented increase in transport rates in recent years. The sector has so far been able to pass on the costs incurred to their customers.

And that is also the picture for 2026. The Dutch road transport sector has seen an unprecedented increase in transport rates in recent years. High inflation has led to a sharp increase in collective labor agreements and higher fuel and financing costs since 2021. The sector has so far been able to pass on the costs incurred to their customers. Tough negotiations do not lead to the optimal transport price.

The Dutch road transport sector has seen an unprecedented increase in transport rates in recent years. High inflation has led to a sharp increase in collective labor agreements and higher fuel and financing costs since 2021. The sector has so far been able to pass on the costs incurred to their customers. Tough negotiations do not lead to the optimal transport price. Transport costs will continue to rise in the coming period.

Transport costs will continue to rise in the coming period. However, it currently appears that clients have become more sensitive to once again rising transport prices.

They also have to deal with higher wage and raw material costs, as well as uncertain economic conditions. This leads to a noticeably tougher stance in the negotiations. This may give clients the lowest price in the short term, but it may actually cost them more in the long term.

Exceptionally increased transport prices are explainable. According to calculations by [research agency Panteia](https://panteia.nl/actueel/nieuws/kostenontwikkelingen-20242025/#:~:text=De%20verwachte%20kostenontwikkeling%2C%20exclusief%20brandstofkost anddevelopment%2C%20for%202025%20ten,%E2%80%9Ccost developments%20in%20the%20road transport%202024-2025%E2%80%9D%20of%20research agency%20Panteia.) the costs for road transport (excluding fuel costs) have increased on average between 25 and 30 percent between 2021 and 2025.

Exceptionally increased transport prices are explainable. According to calculations by [research agency Panteia](https://panteia.nl/actueel/nieuws/kostenontwikkelingen-20242025/#:~:text=De%20verwachte%20kostenontwikkeling%2C%20exclusief%20brandstofkost anddevelopment%2C%20for%202025%20ten,%E2%80%9Ccost developments%20in%20the%20road transport%202024-2025%E2%80%9D%20of%20research agency%20Panteia.) the costs for road transport (excluding fuel costs) have increased on average between 25 and 30 percent between 2021 and 2025. This is significantly higher than the increase of 6 to 12 percent between 2017 and 2021.

Exceptionally increased transport prices are explainable. According to calculations by [research agency Panteia](https://panteia.nl/actueel/nieuws/kostenontwikkelingen-20242025/#:~:text=De%20verwachte%20kostenontwikkeling%2C%20exclusief%20brandstofkost anddevelopment%2C%20for%202025%20ten,%E2%80%9Ccost developments%20in%20the%20road transport%202024-2025%E2%80%9D%20of%20research agency%20Panteia.) the costs for road transport (excluding fuel costs) have increased on average between 25 and 30 percent between 2021 and 2025. This is significantly higher than the increase of 6 to 12 percent between 2017 and 2021. Analysis shows that rates in national road transport have risen faster than in international road transport.

Sector Banker Transport & Logistics
Exceptionally increased transport prices are explainable. According to calculations by [research agency Panteia](https://panteia.nl/actueel/nieuws/kostenontwikkelingen-20242025/#:~:text=De%20verwachte%20kostenontwikkeling%2C%20exclusief%20brandstofkost anddevelopment%2C%20for%202025%20ten,%E2%80%9Ccost developments%20in%20the%20road transport%202024-2025%E2%80%9D%20of%20research agency%20Panteia.) the costs for road transport (excluding fuel costs) have increased on average between 25 and 30 percent between 2021 and 2025. This is significantly higher than the increase of 6 to 12 percent between 2017 and 2021. Analysis shows that rates in national road transport have risen faster than in international road transport. This is mainly due to the higher wage cost increases in the Netherlands.

According to calculations by [research agency Panteia](https://panteia.nl/actueel/nieuws/kostenontwikkelingen-20242025/#:~:text=De%20verwachte%20kostenontwikkeling%2C%20exclusief%20brandstofkost anddevelopment%2C%20for%202025%20ten,%E2%80%9Ccost developments%20in%20the%20road transport%202024-2025%E2%80%9D%20of%20research agency%20Panteia.) the costs for road transport (excluding fuel costs) have increased on average between 25 and 30 percent between 2021 and 2025. This is significantly higher than the increase of 6 to 12 percent between 2017 and 2021. Analysis shows that rates in national road transport have risen faster than in international road transport. This is mainly due to the higher wage cost increases in the Netherlands. Within road transport, prices in parcel transport have risen faster than in container transport.

This is significantly higher than the increase of 6 to 12 percent between 2017 and 2021. Analysis shows that rates in national road transport have risen faster than in international road transport. This is mainly due to the higher wage cost increases in the Netherlands. Within road transport, prices in parcel transport have risen faster than in container transport. The road transport sector has been able to almost fully pass on the cost increase in the period 2012-2024, helped by capacity shortages in transport.

Analysis shows that rates in national road transport have risen faster than in international road transport. This is mainly due to the higher wage cost increases in the Netherlands. Within road transport, prices in parcel transport have risen faster than in container transport. The road transport sector has been able to almost fully pass on the cost increase in the period 2012-2024, helped by capacity shortages in transport. In 2025 and also for 2026, there appears to be more capacity available in terms of fleet size and warehouse space.

In 2025 and also for 2026, there appears to be more capacity available in terms of fleet size and warehouse space. That puts pressure on the negotiations. The transport sector cannot ignore new laws and regulations. In addition to the aforementioned cost increases, it will mainly be the government that will provide further upward pressure in the coming period. The announced excise duty increase as of January 1, 2026 was recently postponed by a year by the cabinet, after the temporary reduction due to the energy crisis in 2022.

That puts pressure on the negotiations. The transport sector cannot ignore new laws and regulations. In addition to the aforementioned cost increases, it will mainly be the government that will provide further upward pressure in the coming period. The announced excise duty increase as of January 1, 2026 was recently postponed by a year by the cabinet, after the temporary reduction due to the energy crisis in 2022. But this will result in a cost increase in 2027 of approximately [about 10 euro cents per liter diesel](https://www.nt.nl/wegtransport/2025/04/02/madlener-legt-wegvoer-met-hogere-dieselprijs-op-pijnbank/#:~:text=When%20 the%20excise discount%20on%20fuels%20from%20January%202026,January%20%2726%20Sun%27n%2010%20cent%20per%20liter%20more expensive.).

The transport sector cannot ignore new laws and regulations. In addition to the aforementioned cost increases, it will mainly be the government that will provide further upward pressure in the coming period. The announced excise duty increase as of January 1, 2026 was recently postponed by a year by the cabinet, after the temporary reduction due to the energy crisis in 2022. But this will result in a cost increase in 2027 of approximately [about 10 euro cents per liter diesel](https://www.nt.nl/wegtransport/2025/04/02/madlener-legt-wegvoer-met-hogere-dieselprijs-op-pijnbank/#:~:text=When%20 the%20excise discount%20on%20fuels%20from%20January%202026,January%20%2726%20Sun%27n%2010%20cent%20per%20liter%20more expensive.). Secondly, there is the introduction of the truck tax as of July 1, 2026.

In addition to the aforementioned cost increases, it will mainly be the government that will provide further upward pressure in the coming period. The announced excise duty increase as of January 1, 2026 was recently postponed by a year by the cabinet, after the temporary reduction due to the energy crisis in 2022. But this will result in a cost increase in 2027 of approximately [about 10 euro cents per liter diesel](https://www.nt.nl/wegtransport/2025/04/02/madlener-legt-wegvoer-met-hogere-dieselprijs-op-pijnbank/#:~:text=When%20 the%20excise discount%20on%20fuels%20from%20January%202026,January%20%2726%20Sun%27n%2010%20cent%20per%20liter%20more expensive.). Secondly, there is the introduction of the truck tax as of July 1, 2026. According to the trade organization [Transport and Logistiek Nederland](https://www.tln.nl/actueel/vrachtwagenkosten-start-op-1-juli-2026), this tax will increase the cost price per kilometer by 15 to 18 cents, depending on weight and CO₂ emission class.

The announced excise duty increase as of January 1, 2026 was recently postponed by a year by the cabinet, after the temporary reduction due to the energy crisis in 2022. But this will result in a cost increase in 2027 of approximately [about 10 euro cents per liter diesel](https://www.nt.nl/wegtransport/2025/04/02/madlener-legt-wegvoer-met-hogere-dieselprijs-op-pijnbank/#:~:text=When%20 the%20excise discount%20on%20fuels%20from%20January%202026,January%20%2726%20Sun%27n%2010%20cent%20per%20liter%20more expensive.). Secondly, there is the introduction of the truck tax as of July 1, 2026. According to the trade organization [Transport and Logistiek Nederland](https://www.tln.nl/actueel/vrachtwagenkosten-start-op-1-juli-2026), this tax will increase the cost price per kilometer by 15 to 18 cents, depending on weight and CO₂ emission class. Also important is the fact that the current collective labor agreement expires at the end of 2025; labor costs are on average 50 percent of the costs of a road transport company.

But this will result in a cost increase in 2027 of approximately [about 10 euro cents per liter diesel](https://www.nt.nl/wegtransport/2025/04/02/madlener-legt-wegvoer-met-hogere-dieselprijs-op-pijnbank/#:~:text=When%20 the%20excise discount%20on%20fuels%20from%20January%202026,January%20%2726%20Sun%27n%2010%20cent%20per%20liter%20more expensive.). Secondly, there is the introduction of the truck tax as of July 1, 2026. According to the trade organization [Transport and Logistiek Nederland](https://www.tln.nl/actueel/vrachtwagenkosten-start-op-1-juli-2026), this tax will increase the cost price per kilometer by 15 to 18 cents, depending on weight and CO₂ emission class. Also important is the fact that the current collective labor agreement expires at the end of 2025; labor costs are on average 50 percent of the costs of a road transport company. A [new agreement](https://www.tln.nl/actueel/herversie-cao-overleg) has not yet been concluded, the talks will resume on September 10.

Also important is the fact that the current collective labor agreement expires at the end of 2025; labor costs are on average 50 percent of the costs of a road transport company. A [new agreement](https://www.tln.nl/actueel/herversie-cao-overleg) has not yet been concluded, the talks will resume on September 10. The new collective labor agreement will certainly lead to a further increase in wage costs.

That doesn't feel good and is at odds with major themes that need to be addressed by the sector and their clients.

The new collective labor agreement will certainly lead to a further increase in wage costs. Higher prices are a fact; productivity must increase. These are expensive times, which opens the door to tough negotiations that are mainly focused on price. That doesn't feel good and is at odds with major themes that need to be addressed by the sector and their clients. In concrete terms, consider electrification of equipment to reduce CO₂ emissions, more professional digitalization and the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to make logistics chains more efficient.

Higher prices are a fact; productivity must increase. These are expensive times, which opens the door to tough negotiations that are mainly focused on price. That doesn't feel good and is at odds with major themes that need to be addressed by the sector and their clients. In concrete terms, consider electrification of equipment to reduce CO₂ emissions, more professional digitalization and the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to make logistics chains more efficient. The real question is how transporters and clients, each based on their role, can better control transport costs in the chain in the coming years.

These are expensive times, which opens the door to tough negotiations that are mainly focused on price. That doesn't feel good and is at odds with major themes that need to be addressed by the sector and their clients. In concrete terms, consider electrification of equipment to reduce CO₂ emissions, more professional digitalization and the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to make logistics chains more efficient. The real question is how transporters and clients, each based on their role, can better control transport costs in the chain in the coming years. The answer lies mainly in organizing the chains in a smarter way.

That doesn't feel good and is at odds with major themes that need to be addressed by the sector and their clients. In concrete terms, consider electrification of equipment to reduce CO₂ emissions, more professional digitalization and the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to make logistics chains more efficient. The real question is how transporters and clients, each based on their role, can better control transport costs in the chain in the coming years. The answer lies mainly in organizing the chains in a smarter way. The [report by the consultants of PWC](https://www.pwc.nl/nl/themas/blogs/digitalisering-in-de-nederlandse-transportsector.html#:~:text=Volgens%20eerder%20PwC-onderz (oek%20was%20the%20sector%20in%202022,in%20same%20period%20significant%20productivityimprovements%20have%20shown%20.) of August 2024 states that the Dutch transport and storage sector was no less than 4.3 percent less productive in 2022 than in 2015.

In concrete terms, consider electrification of equipment to reduce CO₂ emissions, more professional digitalization and the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to make logistics chains more efficient. The real question is how transporters and clients, each based on their role, can better control transport costs in the chain in the coming years. The answer lies mainly in organizing the chains in a smarter way. The [report by the consultants of PWC](https://www.pwc.nl/nl/themas/blogs/digitalisering-in-de-nederlandse-transportsector.html#:~:text=Volgens%20eerder%20PwC-onderz (oek%20was%20the%20sector%20in%202022,in%20same%20period%20significant%20productivityimprovements%20have%20shown%20.) of August 2024 states that the Dutch transport and storage sector was no less than 4.3 percent less productive in 2022 than in 2015. To turn the tide, they consider increasing use of digitalization, automation and cooperation between clients and transporters to be promising.

The real question is how transporters and clients, each based on their role, can better control transport costs in the chain in the coming years. The answer lies mainly in organizing the chains in a smarter way. The [report by the consultants of PWC](https://www.pwc.nl/nl/themas/blogs/digitalisering-in-de-nederlandse-transportsector.html#:~:text=Volgens%20eerder%20PwC-onderz (oek%20was%20the%20sector%20in%202022,in%20same%20period%20significant%20productivityimprovements%20have%20shown%20.) of August 2024 states that the Dutch transport and storage sector was no less than 4.3 percent less productive in 2022 than in 2015. To turn the tide, they consider increasing use of digitalization, automation and cooperation between clients and transporters to be promising. This is confirmed by the recent business survey by the Central Bureau of Statistics.

The answer lies mainly in organizing the chains in a smarter way. The [report by the consultants of PWC](https://www.pwc.nl/nl/themas/blogs/digitalisering-in-de-nederlandse-transportsector.html#:~:text=Volgens%20eerder%20PwC-onderz (oek%20was%20the%20sector%20in%202022,in%20same%20period%20significant%20productivityimprovements%20have%20shown%20.) of August 2024 states that the Dutch transport and storage sector was no less than 4.3 percent less productive in 2022 than in 2015. To turn the tide, they consider increasing use of digitalization, automation and cooperation between clients and transporters to be promising. This is confirmed by the recent business survey by the Central Bureau of Statistics. The increase in the number of tonnes transported per driver has increased [productivity in road transport](https://www.transportlogistiek.nl/logistiek/2025/06/vrachtwagendrivers-worden-steeds-productiever/) by 5 percent in 2024.

The [report by the consultants of PWC](https://www.pwc.nl/nl/themas/blogs/digitalisering-in-de-nederlandse-transportsector.html#:~:text=Volgens%20eerder%20PwC-onderz (oek%20was%20the%20sector%20in%202022,in%20same%20period%20significant%20productivityimprovements%20have%20shown%20.) of August 2024 states that the Dutch transport and storage sector was no less than 4.3 percent less productive in 2022 than in 2015. To turn the tide, they consider increasing use of digitalization, automation and cooperation between clients and transporters to be promising. This is confirmed by the recent business survey by the Central Bureau of Statistics. The increase in the number of tonnes transported per driver has increased [productivity in road transport](https://www.transportlogistiek.nl/logistiek/2025/06/vrachtwagendrivers-worden-steeds-productiever/) by 5 percent in 2024. According to Statistics Netherlands, the reasons for this are a better load factor and fewer empty transport kilometers, driven by a driver shortage and investments in digital planning tools and automation. > “The answer mainly lies in organizing the chains in a smarter way”.

According to Statistics Netherlands, the reasons for this are a better load factor and fewer empty transport kilometers, driven by a driver shortage and investments in digital planning tools and automation. > “The answer mainly lies in organizing the chains in a smarter way”. The use of artificial intelligence has a lot of potential. AI will also help the sector. Artificial intelligence was central to the event surrounding the [Top 100 Logistics Service Providers 2025](https://www.logistiek.nl/203750/top-100-logistiek-dienstproviders-2025-wie-staat-waar-in-de-25e-editie).

Bart Banning. Sector Banker Transport & Logistics
The use of artificial intelligence has a lot of potential. AI will also help the sector. Artificial intelligence was central to the event surrounding the [Top 100 Logistics Service Providers 2025](https://www.logistiek.nl/203750/top-100-logistiek-dienstproviders-2025-wie-staat-waar-in-de-25e-editie). According to consultant [CGI](https://www.cgi.com/nl/nl/artikelen/artificial-intelligence/de-application-van-ai-de-logistiek-groeit-terecht-heel-snel), a rapidly increasing number of companies have already started doing this in one way or another.

Sector Banker Transport & Logistics
The use of artificial intelligence has a lot of potential. AI will also help the sector. Artificial intelligence was central to the event surrounding the [Top 100 Logistics Service Providers 2025](https://www.logistiek.nl/203750/top-100-logistiek-dienstproviders-2025-wie-staat-waar-in-de-25e-editie). According to consultant [CGI](https://www.cgi.com/nl/nl/artikelen/artificial-intelligence/de-application-van-ai-de-logistiek-groeit-terecht-heel-snel), a rapidly increasing number of companies have already started doing this in one way or another. About 75 percent of the top 20, and 40 percent of the rest, appear to be working on applications such as chatbots, automatic reporting and/or customer service.

AI will also help the sector. Artificial intelligence was central to the event surrounding the [Top 100 Logistics Service Providers 2025](https://www.logistiek.nl/203750/top-100-logistiek-dienstproviders-2025-wie-staat-waar-in-de-25e-editie). According to consultant [CGI](https://www.cgi.com/nl/nl/artikelen/artificial-intelligence/de-application-van-ai-de-logistiek-groeit-terecht-heel-snel), a rapidly increasing number of companies have already started doing this in one way or another. About 75 percent of the top 20, and 40 percent of the rest, appear to be working on applications such as chatbots, automatic reporting and/or customer service. In the [video series of Logistiek.nl](https://www.logistiek.nl/204318/nunner-logistics-versnelt-groei-met-slimme-ai-systemen-video) about AI, van der Wal Transport shows the added value of AI in the planning process, a crucial instrument for companies in this sector.

After a difficult year, in which international trade declined due to a rapid reduction of excess inventories, the transport sector has started a cautious recovery. After a difficult year, in which international trade declined due to a rapid reduction of excess inventories, the transport sector has started a cautious recovery. At the beginning of 2024, freight volumes increased in both container shipping and aviation. Thanks to the improving economic growth, the sector can recover further. However, the recovery is being held back by lower demand for building materials. In the meantime, road transport will have to deal with a pile-up of environmental regulations in the coming years.
